name: Q382459
abstract: John Stubblefield (February 4, 1945 – July 4, 2005) was an American jazz saxophonist, flautist, and oboist.

concerts.label: New York Jazz Repertory Company: Jazz With a Spanish Tinge

about_quotes.speaker: Chico Freeman
about_quotes.evidence: Then Cecil McBee came and I sat in with Cecil McBee and John Stubblefield... I was playing with his band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Chico Freeman interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 13, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Barron
about_quotes.evidence: Then, also on Enja Records, right after that, the next year, What If?, with Wallace Roney, John Stubblefield, Cecil McBee, and Victor Lewis... [INTERVIEWEE] Yeah, one of mine too. That record, I had gotten a grant from the National Endowment to put together a group and do some live concerts and stuf
about_quotes.doc_title: Kenny Barron — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Owens
about_quotes.evidence: I’m friends and working with Billy Taylor and I’m in the quintet... It was usually the saxophonist, Frank Wess or Selman Powell, or, later on, John Stubblefield or Jimmy Heath. I was always there as a trumpet player.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Owens — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Tyner McCoy
about_quotes.evidence: INTERVIEWER: ...John Stubblefield, Joe Ford, so you brought in- [INTERVIEWEE] The older guys.
about_quotes.doc_title: Tyner McCoy — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Liebman
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] ... John Stubblefield ... [INTERVIEWEE] I didn't know that.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Liebman — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
